# Jira CLI

Interact with Atlassian Jira from the command line using [jira-cli](https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli).

## When to Use

- User asks to create, view, edit, or search Jira issues/tickets
- User needs to transition issues through workflow states (To Do → In Progress → Done)
- User wants to manage sprints, epics, or boards
- User needs to assign issues, add comments, or log work time
- User asks about their current tasks or sprint progress

## Prerequisites

1. Install jira-cli: `brew install ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/jira-cli` (macOS) or download from [releases](https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/releases)
2. Set API token: `export JIRA_API_TOKEN="your-token"`
3. Initialize: `jira init` and follow prompts

## Issue Commands

### List Issues

```bash
# List issues in current project
jira issue list

# List my assigned issues
jira issue list -a$(jira me)

# List issues by status
jira issue list -s"In Progress"

# List high priority issues
jira issue list -yHigh

# List issues with multiple filters
jira issue list -a$(jira me) -s"To Do" -yHigh --created week

# List issues with raw JQL
jira issue list -q "project = PROJ AND status = 'In Progress'"

# Plain text output for scripting
jira issue list --plain --columns key,summary,status --no-headers
```

### Create Issues

```bash
# Interactive issue creation
jira issue create

# Create with all options specified
jira issue create -tBug -s"Login button not working" -b"Description here" -yHigh --no-input

# Create a story
jira issue create -tStory -s"Add user authentication" -yMedium

# Create with labels and components
jira issue create -tTask -s"Update dependencies" -lmaintenance -l"tech-debt" -Cbackend

# Create and assign to self
jira issue create -tBug -s"Fix crash on startup" -a$(jira me) --no-input
```

### View Issues

```bash
# View issue details
jira issue view ISSUE-123

# View with comments
jira issue view ISSUE-123 --comments 10

# View in plain text
jira issue view ISSUE-123 --plain

# Open issue in browser
jira open ISSUE-123
```

### Edit Issues

```bash
# Edit summary
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -s"Updated summary"

# Edit description
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -b"New description"

# Edit priority
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -yHigh

# Add labels
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -lnew-label
```

### Transition Issues

```bash
# Move issue to a new status
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "In Progress"

# Move with comment
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" --comment "Completed the task"

# Move and set resolution
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" -RFixed
```

### Assign Issues

```bash
# Assign to self
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 $(jira me)

# Assign to specific user
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 username

# Unassign
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 x
```

### Comments

```bash
# Add a comment
jira issue comment add ISSUE-123 "This is my comment"

# Add comment from editor
jira issue comment add ISSUE-123
```

### Work Logging

```bash
# Log time
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "2h 30m"

# Log time with comment
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "1d 4h" --comment "Completed feature implementation" --no-input
```

### Link & Clone Issues

```bash
# Link two issues
jira issue link ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456 Blocks

# Unlink issues
jira issue unlink ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456

# Clone an issue
jira issue clone ISSUE-123 -s"Cloned: New summary"

# Delete an issue
jira issue delete ISSUE-123
```

## Epic Commands

```bash
# List epics
jira epic list

# List epics in table format
jira epic list --table

# Create an epic
jira epic create -n"Q1 Features" -s"Epic summary" -b"Epic description"

# Add issues to epic
jira epic add EPIC-1 ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456

# Remove issues from epic
jira epic remove ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456
```

## Sprint Commands

```bash
# List sprints
jira sprint list

# List current/active sprint
jira sprint list --current

# List my issues in current sprint
jira sprint list --current -a$(jira me)

# Add issues to sprint
jira sprint add SPRINT_ID ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456
```

## Project & Board Commands

```bash
# List projects
jira project list

# List boards
jira board list

# List releases/versions
jira release list

# Open project in browser
jira open
```

## Utility Commands

```bash
# Get current username
jira me

# Show help
jira --help
jira issue --help

# Setup shell completion
jira completion bash  # or zsh, fish, powershell
```

## Common Flags

| Flag | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `--plain` | Plain text output (no interactive UI) |
| `--raw` | Raw JSON output |
| `--csv` | CSV output |
| `--no-input` | Skip interactive prompts |
| `-t, --type` | Issue type (Bug, Story, Task, Epic) |
| `-s, --summary` | Issue summary/title |
| `-b, --body` | Issue description |
| `-y, --priority` | Priority (Highest, High, Medium, Low, Lowest) |
| `-l, --label` | Labels (repeatable) |
| `-a, --assignee` | Assignee username |
| `-r, --reporter` | Reporter username |
| `-C, --component` | Component name |
| `-P, --parent` | Parent issue/epic key |
| `-q, --jql` | Raw JQL query |
| `--created` | Filter by creation date (-7d, week, month) |
| `--order-by` | Sort field |
| `--reverse` | Reverse sort order |

## Common Workflows

### Start Working on an Issue

```bash
# Assign to self and move to In Progress
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 $(jira me)
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "In Progress"
```

### Complete an Issue

```bash
# Log work and close
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "4h" --no-input
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" --comment "Completed" -RFixed
```

### Daily Standup Review

```bash
# View my current sprint tasks
jira sprint list --current -a$(jira me)
```

### Create and Track a Bug

```bash
# Create bug
jira issue create -tBug -s"App crashes on login" -yHigh -lbug --no-input
# Note the returned issue key, then assign
jira issue assign BUG-123 $(jira me)
jira issue move BUG-123 "In Progress"
```

## Output Examples

| Command | Use Case |
| ------- | -------- |
| `jira issue list --plain` | Script-friendly output |
| `jira issue list --raw` | JSON for parsing |
| `jira issue list --csv` | Export to spreadsheet |

## Limitations

- Requires prior `jira init` configuration
- Some features may vary between Jira Cloud and Server
- Complex custom fields may require `--custom` flag with field IDs
- Rate limits apply based on Jira instance configuration

  ## Description                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  Create the PR Refinement agent: a daily cron job that reads inline comments posted by the PR Review agent and pushes
  back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal.                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  Three files to create:                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md — modeled on agents/pr-review/prompt.md                                            
  • agents/pr-refinement/README.md — mirrors agents/pr-review/README.md in structure                                  
  • .github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml — daily cron sweep (not event-triggered); scans all open PRs that already have
  pr-review inline comments                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  Note: pr-review ( .github/workflows/pr-review.yml ) triggers on pull_request events; refinement runs its own daily  
  cron.                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  The PR Review + PR Refinement pair acts as a deliberate adversarial check: one model flags issues, a different model
  pushes back. This hedge against single-model review bias is a named design choice in docs/agentic-workflow.md       
  (Design                                                                                                             
  choices). The pr-review workflow already exists at .github/workflows/pr-review.yml ; refinement is its complement.  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Implementation notes                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  **Model choice:** Use a different Claude model than pr-review's default ( anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 with no --
  model override). Specify via claude_args: --model ❬model-id❭ in the workflow. Verify current model IDs against      
  Anthropic docs and document the choice and rationale in agents/pr-refinement/README.md .                            
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • A PR that has pr-review inline comments receives a refinement pass that selectively pushes back on specific       
  comments.                                                                                                           
  • A PR that has reached the round cap is skipped in subsequent daily runs.                                          
  • Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-❬PR_NUMBER❭/❬RUN_ID❭.json — matching the convention in
  the "Commit execution log" step of .github/workflows/pr-review.yml .                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Which stop condition should the refinement agent use to prevent re-processing the same PR indefinitely: (A)     
  store a round counter as a PR label (e.g. refinement-rounds:N ) and skip the PR once it reaches a cap, or (B) freeze
  once a human has posted on the PR?                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ 1 Comments ------------------------                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  Jae Yoon Bae • Thu, 11 Jun 26 • Latest comment                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  Yes, maybe let's try to track how many times it was refined, and then, based on that, stop. For now, I think one    
  round would be sufficient, but it should be variable.

You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input. Don't use
  `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons
  against main have no merge base and will fail.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` to avoid shell interpretation.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

1	# PR Review agent
2	
3	Daily/per-PR reviewer. Reads the PR diff, posts inline comments with severity
4	and reasoning, optionally a short top-level summary. Comment-only — never
5	approves, merges, or pushes code.
6	
7	Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).
8	
9	## Files
10	
11	- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
12	- `../../.github/workflows/pr-review.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.
13	
14	## How it runs
15	
16	The workflow `pr-review.yml` triggers on pull_request events
17	(`opened`, `synchronize`, `ready_for_review`, `reopened`). It checks out the
18	PR, prepends repo and PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to
19	`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action installs Claude Code on the
20	runner and runs a headless session in the working directory.
21	
22	`track_progress: true` is enabled — the action posts a sticky tracking
23	comment ("Claude Code is reviewing this pull request…") and updates it as
24	work progresses.
25	
26	## Manual run
27	
28	```sh
29	gh workflow run pr-review.yml
30	```
31	
32	This uses the `workflow_dispatch` trigger and runs against the default branch.
33	For a real review, push to a PR — the `pull_request` trigger fires
34	automatically.
35	
36	## Editing the prompt
37	
38	Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change to
39	the prompt takes effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.
40	
41	## What's deliberately not configured
42	
43	- No `--model` — uses the action's default.
44	- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
45	- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
46	- No write access to `contents:` — review-only by construction.
47	
48	## Required setup (one-time)
49	
50	Authenticated via Claude Pro/Max OAuth (uses your subscription quota, not
51	per-token API billing):
52	
53	1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
54	   it prints a long-lived OAuth token.
55	2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
56	   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.
57	
58	Heads up: CI runs draw from the same 5-hour usage buckets as your local
59	Claude Code sessions. If you'd rather use per-token API billing, swap
60	`claude_code_oauth_token:` for `anthropic_api_key:` in the workflow and use
61	an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret instead.
62

# PR Review agent

Daily/per-PR reviewer. Reads the PR diff, posts inline comments with severity
and reasoning, optionally a short top-level summary. Comment-only — never
approves, merges, or pushes code.

Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).

## Files

- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
- `../../.github/workflows/pr-review.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.

## How it runs

The workflow `pr-review.yml` triggers on pull_request events
(`opened`, `synchronize`, `ready_for_review`, `reopened`). It checks out the
PR, prepends repo and PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to
`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action installs Claude Code on the
runner and runs a headless session in the working directory.

`track_progress: true` is enabled — the action posts a sticky tracking
comment ("Claude Code is reviewing this pull request…") and updates it as
work progresses.

## Manual run

```sh
gh workflow run pr-review.yml
```

This uses the `workflow_dispatch` trigger and runs against the default branch.
For a real review, push to a PR — the `pull_request` trigger fires
automatically.

## Editing the prompt

Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change to
the prompt takes effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.

## What's deliberately not configured

- No `--model` — uses the action's default.
- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
- No write access to `contents:` — review-only by construction.

## Required setup (one-time)

Authenticated via Claude Pro/Max OAuth (uses your subscription quota, not
per-token API billing):

1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
   it prints a long-lived OAuth token.
2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.

Heads up: CI runs draw from the same 5-hour usage buckets as your local
Claude Code sessions. If you'd rather use per-token API billing, swap
`claude_code_oauth_token:` for `anthropic_api_key:` in the workflow and use
an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret instead.

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

15	        ▼
16	   Ticket Agent
17	        │
18	        ▼
19	Development Orchestrator
20	        │
21	        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
22	        │                          │

33	                                  │   (headless `claude -p`, --max-turns)

36	                          (human picks up &

38	```
39	
40	## Human touchpoints
41	
42	- **Start:** humans create tickets.
43	- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
44	- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
45	  into the loop before implementing.
46	
47	## Agents
48	
49	### Ticket Agent
50	**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.
51	
52	- Does **not** invent new tickets.
53	- Works on a daily basis. 
54	- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
55	- Creates dependencies between tickets (`blocks`, `depends on`).
56	
57	### Development Orchestrator
58	**Goal:** decide which development agent to launch for each ticket.
59	
60	- Reads the ticket.
61	- Decides the complexity (complex vs. easy) and routes accordingly.
62	
63	### Senior Developer Agent — `/openspec:explore`
64	**Goal:** tackle complex tasks **with a human in the loop**.
65	
66	- Uses spec-driven development.
67	- Requests human input before moving to implementation.
68	- Same runtime shape as Junior: headless `claude -p` on a GHA runner with
69	  `--max-turns` and a workflow timeout. The `/openspec:explore` skill and
70	  human-in-the-loop pauses layer on top of that base.
71

73	**Goal:** tackle easy tasks **without a human in the loop**.
74	
75	- Headless `claude -p` invoked directly from a GitHub Actions workflow
76	  (no skill wrapper). Plans straight through to execution.
77	- Bounded by `--max-turns` per dispatch + a workflow-level `timeout-minutes`.
78	- Creates a Draft PR; a human picks it up from there.
79	
80	### PR Review Agent
81	**Goal:** daily review of updated PRs.
82	
83	- Inline comments.
84	- Severity.
85	- Reasoning.
86	
87	### PR Refinement Agent
88	**Goal:** daily refinement of the PR.
89	
90	- May use a different model (e.g. CODEX).
91	- Counteracts the review (acts as a deliberate counter-voice to the reviewer).
92

96	  human-in-the-loop cost only on work that needs it.
97	- **Runtime split.** `claude-code-action` for read/triage surfaces
98	  (orchestrator, ticket grooming, PR review, agent-improver); raw

115	- How are the two daily passes (review + refinement) sequenced — same run, or
116	  staggered?

# Agentic Development Workflow

> **Status:** First-draft vision. Extracted from a hand-drawn diagram (2026-06-04).
> Not yet scoped, designed, or implemented.

The flow describes an end-to-end pipeline where humans create tickets and merge
final PRs, and a chain of agents handles everything in between: ticket grooming,
complexity routing, implementation, testing, review, and refinement.

## High-level flow

```
Humans create tickets
        │
        ▼
   Ticket Agent
        │
        ▼
Development Orchestrator
        │
        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
        │                          │
        │                          ▼
        │                    PR Review agent ◄────┐
        │                          ▲              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                    PR Refinement agent  │
        │                          │              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                Humans test & merge PR   │
        │                                         │
        └──── easy ──────► Junior developer agent │
                                  │   (headless `claude -p`, --max-turns)
                                  ▼               │
                              Draft PR ───────────┘
                          (human picks up &
                           merges, or re-runs)
```

## Human touchpoints

- **Start:** humans create tickets.
- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
  into the loop before implementing.

## Agents

### Ticket Agent
**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.

- Does **not** invent new tickets.
- Works on a daily basis. 
- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
- Creates dependencies between tickets (`blocks`, `depends on`).

### Development Orchestrator
**Goal:** decide which development agent to launch for each ticket.

- Reads the ticket.
- Decides the complexity (complex vs. easy) and routes accordingly.

### Senior Developer Agent — `/openspec:explore`
**Goal:** tackle complex tasks **with a human in the loop**.

- Uses spec-driven development.
- Requests human input before moving to implementation.
- Same runtime shape as Junior: headless `claude -p` on a GHA runner with
  `--max-turns` and a workflow timeout. The `/openspec:explore` skill and
  human-in-the-loop pauses layer on top of that base.

### Junior Developer Agent
**Goal:** tackle easy tasks **without a human in the loop**.

- Headless `claude -p` invoked directly from a GitHub Actions workflow
  (no skill wrapper). Plans straight through to execution.
- Bounded by `--max-turns` per dispatch + a workflow-level `timeout-minutes`.
- Creates a Draft PR; a human picks it up from there.

### PR Review Agent
**Goal:** daily review of updated PRs.

- Inline comments.
- Severity.
- Reasoning.

### PR Refinement Agent
**Goal:** daily refinement of the PR.

- May use a different model (e.g. CODEX).
- Counteracts the review (acts as a deliberate counter-voice to the reviewer).

## Design choices worth calling out

- **Complexity gate at the orchestrator.** Two-tier routing keeps the
  human-in-the-loop cost only on work that needs it.
- **Runtime split.** `claude-code-action` for read/triage surfaces
  (orchestrator, ticket grooming, PR review, agent-improver); raw
  `claude -p` for implementation lanes (junior-dev, senior-dev). The
  action's PR-comment plumbing is dead weight when the agent's job is to
  edit files, push a branch, and open a PR.
- **Bounded dev-agent runs.** `--max-turns` per dispatch + `timeout-minutes`
  on the job stop a hard ticket from spinning forever. Tickets stay in
  **In Progress** so the orchestrator won't re-fire them.
- **Review ↔ Refinement as an adversarial pair.** Using a different model for
  refinement is a deliberate hedge against single-model review bias.
- **Daily cadence** on review and refinement rather than per-PR-event triggers.

## Open questions (for follow-up)

- How is "complex vs. easy" actually decided? Heuristic, classifier, or LLM
  judgment with calibration?
- Where does the orchestrator live (cron, webhook, queue)?
- How does the Refinement agent know when to stop counteracting?
- How are the two daily passes (review + refinement) sequenced — same run, or
  staggered?

You are the **PR Refinement agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Act as a deliberate counter-voice to the PR Review agent. Read the inline comments
already posted by the PR Review agent on this PR and push back selectively on those
that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal. Nothing else.

## What to push back on

Challenge a comment when it meets one or more of these conditions:

1. **Wrong fact** — the reviewer flagged something based on incorrect or outdated
   information about a library API, language behavior, or external tool.
2. **Overstated severity** — a `High` or `Critical` rating that should be `Medium`
   or `Low` given the actual impact; or a `Medium` that is not a real issue.
3. **Low-signal nit** — style preference, naming bikeshed, hypothetical future
   requirement, or a "have you considered…" musing with no concrete problem stated.
4. **False positive** — the surrounding context (PR intent, adjacent code, tests,
   existing conventions in the repo) shows the flagged code is correct and
   intentional.

Do **not** push back on:
- Clear correctness bugs, even minor ones.
- Security issues with a concrete and plausible attack path.
- Performance concerns that are measurably significant in the actual usage pattern.

When in doubt, stay silent. Only push back when you have a concrete reason.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — PR metadata.
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the full diff; primary input.
- `gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments` — all inline review comments.
  Filter for those posted by `github-actions[bot]` whose body contains `**Severity:**` —
  those are the PR Review agent's comments. Note each comment's `id` field.
- Read files in the working directory to understand context around flagged lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, changelogs) — `WebSearch`, then
  `WebFetch` for specific pages. When fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub
  source over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If verification confirms the reviewer's claim, leave the comment alone.

## How to post pushback

Reply directly to a specific inline comment via:

```sh
gh api "repos/${REPO}/pulls/comments/${COMMENT_ID}/replies" -f body="..."
```

Each reply must be concise:
- One clear sentence stating why the comment is wrong, over-eager, or low-signal.
- One sentence of concrete evidence: a code reference, a verified API doc, or a
  repo convention that directly supports your pushback.

Do **not** post a top-level "LGTM" or blanket response. If every review comment
is valid, post nothing and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not
recap which replies you posted. The runner will exit.

input.content:
# PR Refinement agent

Daily counter-voice to the PR Review agent. Reads inline comments posted by the
PR Review agent and pushes back selectively on those that are wrong, over-eager,
or low-signal. Reply-only — never approves, merges, or pushes code.

Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).

## Files

- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
- `../../.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.

## How it runs

The workflow `pr-refinement.yml` runs on a daily cron schedule. A `discover` job
finds all open PRs that:

1. Already have at least one inline comment from the PR Review agent (identified
   by `github-actions[bot]` authorship and the `**Severity:**` marker in the body).
2. Have not yet reached the configured round cap (`MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS`,
   defaulting to `1`).

For each eligible PR, a matrix `refine` job checks out the PR head, prepends repo
and PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`.

**Model:** `claude-opus-4-8` — a different model than PR Review's default
(the action's built-in model, no `--model` override) to hedge against
single-model review bias. See [Design choices](#design-choices).

## Round cap (stop condition)

After each refinement pass the workflow adds or updates a label
`refinement-rounds:N` on the PR, where `N` is the total completed rounds. On
the next daily run, PRs where `N ≥ MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` are skipped.

`MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` defaults to `1`. Override by setting a repository
variable of the same name (**Settings → Variables → Actions**).

## Manual run

```sh
gh workflow run pr-refinement.yml
```

Runs against all currently eligible open PRs.

## Editing the prompt

Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so changes take
effect on the next run without any other plumbing.

## Design choices

- **Different model than PR Review.** PR Review uses `claude-code-action@v1`
  with no `--model` override. PR Refinement pins `claude-opus-4-8` so the
  counter-voice comes from a genuinely independent model — a named design
  choice in `docs/agentic-workflow.md` ("Review ↔ Refinement as an adversarial
  pair").
- **Cron, not event-triggered.** Refinement runs once daily rather than firing
  on every new review comment, giving reviewer comments time to settle before
  the counter-pass.
- **Selective pushback only.** The agent replies only to comments it can
  concretely refute; it posts no summaries, blanket LGTMs, or top-level
  comments.
- **Label-based round tracking.** `refinement-rounds:N` labels are visible
  directly in the PR UI, making the stop condition auditable without querying
  the API.

## What's deliberately not configured

- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
- `track_progress: false` — no sticky tracking comment clutters the PR thread.

## Required setup (one-time)

Same credentials as PR Review — see [`agents/pr-review/README.md`](../pr-review/README.md)
for full details.

- `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` repository secret (Claude Pro/Max OAuth) **or**
  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` for per-token billing.
- `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` repository secret for library doc lookups.

input.content:
name: PR Refinement agent

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  discover:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      matrix: ${{ steps.find-prs.outputs.matrix }}
    env:
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS: ${{ vars.MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS || '1' }}
    steps:
      - name: Find PRs eligible for refinement
        id: find-prs
        run: |
          set -e
          REPO="${{ github.repository }}"
          MAX_ROUNDS="${MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS}"

          PR_DATA=$(gh pr list --repo "$REPO" --state open --json number,headRefName --limit 100)
          PR_COUNT=$(echo "$PR_DATA" | jq length)

          MATRIX_ITEMS="[]"

          for i in $(seq 0 $((PR_COUNT - 1))); do
            PR_NUMBER=$(echo "$PR_DATA" | jq -r ".[$i].number")
            HEAD_REF=$(echo "$PR_DATA" | jq -r ".[$i].headRefName")

            # Skip PRs that have no pr-review inline comments
            REVIEW_COMMENT_COUNT=$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments" \
              --jq '[.[] | select(.user.login == "github-actions[bot]") | select(.body | test("\\*\\*Severity:\\*\\*"))] | length')

            if [ "$REVIEW_COMMENT_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
              continue
            fi

            # Skip PRs that have reached the round cap
            CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$REPO" \
              --json labels \
              --jq '[.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))] | if length > 0 then .[0] | split(":")[1] | tonumber else 0 end')

            if [ "$CURRENT_ROUNDS" -ge "$MAX_ROUNDS" ]; then
              continue
            fi

            MATRIX_ITEMS=$(echo "$MATRIX_ITEMS" | jq \
              --argjson n "$PR_NUMBER" \
              --arg r "$HEAD_REF" \
              '. + [{"number": $n, "head_ref": $r}]')
          done

          echo "matrix=${MATRIX_ITEMS}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  refine:
    needs: discover
    if: needs.discover.outputs.matrix != '[]' && needs.discover.outputs.matrix != ''
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover.outputs.matrix) }}
      max-parallel: 3
      fail-fast: false
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ matrix.head_ref }}
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ matrix.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --model claude-opus-4-8 --allowedTools "mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh api repos*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Increment refinement-rounds label
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.number }}
        run: |
          set -e
          REPO="${{ github.repository }}"

          CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$REPO" \
            --json labels \
            --jq '[.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))] | if length > 0 then .[0] | split(":")[1] | tonumber else 0 end')

          NEW_ROUNDS=$((CURRENT_ROUNDS + 1))

          if [ "$CURRENT_ROUNDS" -gt 0 ]; then
            gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$REPO" --remove-label "refinement-rounds:${CURRENT_ROUNDS}" || true
          fi

          gh label create "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}" \
            --repo "$REPO" \
            --color "0075ca" \
            --description "PR has been through ${NEW_ROUNDS} refinement round(s)" || true

          gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$REPO" --add-label "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.number }}
          HEAD_REF: ${{ matrix.head_ref }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$HEAD_REF"
          git checkout -B "$HEAD_REF" "origin/$HEAD_REF"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: pr-refinement PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$HEAD_REF"

gh pr create --draft --base main --title "[KAN-5] Build PR Refinement agent (counter-voice to PR Review)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Adds `agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md`: instructs the agent to act as a deliberate counter-voice, pushing back only on review comments that are factually wrong, overstated in severity, low-signal nits, or false positives — with concrete evidence required for each reply.
- Adds `agents/pr-refinement/README.md`: documents the model choice, round-cap stop condition, and adversarial-pair design rationale.
- Adds `.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml`: daily cron (`0 2 * * *`) that discovers eligible open PRs (have pr-review inline comments, below `MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS`), runs each through `claude-opus-4-8` via `claude-code-action@v1`, tracks rounds with `refinement-rounds:N` PR labels, and commits execution logs to `.github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-{PR_NUMBER}/{RUN_ID}.json`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] A PR that has pr-review inline comments receives a refinement pass that selectively pushes back on specific comments.
- [x] A PR that has reached the round cap (`refinement-rounds:N ≥ MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS`) is skipped in subsequent daily runs. The cap defaults to `1` and is configurable via the `MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` repository variable.
- [x] Run log committed under `.github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-{PR_NUMBER}/{RUN_ID}.json` — matching the convention in the "Commit execution log" step of `.github/workflows/pr-review.yml`.

## Jira

https://jaeyoonsworkspace-23358853.atlassian.net/browse/KAN-5
EOF
